content: Chemotherapy administration follows evidence-based protocols. Dosing calculated by body surface area (BSA) or weight; renal/hepatic adjustment required for many agents. Cycles typically every 1-3 weeks allowing bone marrow recovery. Common regimens: FOLFOX (colon cancer), AC-T (breast cancer), CHOP (lymphoma), BEP (testicular cancer). Pre-medications prevent reactions: dexamethasone, ondansetron, aprepitant for nausea; diphenhydramine for hypersensitivity. Growth factor support (G-CSF) reduces neutropenia risk when febrile neutropenia risk >20%. Dose modifications: reduce 20-25% for grade 3-4 toxicity; delay until counts recover (ANC >1500, platelets >100,000). Toxicity management: antiemetics per emetogenic risk; mucosal care for stomatitis; loperamide for diarrhea; erythropoietin for anemia (controversial). Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with allopurinol/rasburicase for high-burden hematologic malignancies.
